reasoning: Query is 宿坊 (temple lodging). Top result is a Chinese-language ryokan page unrelated to temple lodging. Second result is a Nagano tourism article about temple lodging culture (Gyokusho-in). Wikidata results are entirely unrelated (東尋坊 cliff, 包丁正宗 sword, 妻籠宿 post town). No actual shukubo entities returned. Recall partial via article. Prominence severely wrong — cliff/sword rank above temple-lodging content.
missing_entities: actual shukubo establishments (Koya-san shukubo, Shogi-in, etc.)
wrong_facts: 東尋坊 and 包丁正宗 are not related to 宿坊 at all; appearing in results indicates poor relevance filtering
improvement_hint: Add 宿坊 as a recognized kind/type tag; boost wikidata entities with description matching temple/lodging; filter out noise results like cliff landforms and swords for this query

reasoning: Query is about quiet hot spring villages for yukata walks (温泉街). Top result is 温泉津温泉 (Yunotsu Onsen, Shimane) with prominence_score=0.85, coordinates, heritage_designations including Traditional Buildings district — highly relevant and well-structured. Subsequent results include temple and mountain named 温泉, which are off-topic. Famous onsen-machi like 城崎, 草津, 下呂 are missing. Wikidata schema is good with qid, coords, heritage_designations, prominence_score.
missing_entities: 城崎温泉, 草津温泉, 下呂温泉, 別府温泉街

reasoning: Query is about deep tea ceremony experience in Kyoto; tool used is get_japan_heritage(Kyoto). Response returns Japan Heritage stories — Lake Biwa Canal and Tango Chirimen Corridor — both accurate and well-sourced with bunka.go.jp URLs, bilingual content, themes/periods metadata. However, neither story is specifically about tea ceremony (茶道). No Urasenke, Omotesenke, or tea culture heritage stories surface. Traceability is excellent with official source URLs and translation_meta.

reasoning: Query is mountain villages with rare local train service; search term ローカル. Top results are off-domain: a link policy page, a crowding info page, an Important Cultural Property (旧羅典神学校), a Hokkaido video library, and a DMO plan chunk mentioning ローカル railroad. reasoning: get_local_food returns 500 of 24262 GI-designated foods with full structured records: key, source_url (maff.go.jp), bilingual name and description, registration_date, prefecture_codes, production_area_text, translation_meta. Aomori Cassis (#1), Tajima Beef (#2), Yubari Melon (#4), Yame Gyokuro (#5) are all real, accurate GI products. truncation_note correctly warns 500/24262. Minus: ordering starts from registration number 1 (chronological), not by niche/obscurity relevance to query. Well-known products (Yubari Melon) appear before truly lesser-known ones.
improvement_hint: Add relevance re-ranking for get_local_food so 'lesser-known' queries deprioritize top-registration-number (earliest/most famous) items; expose a popularity_rank or visibility_score field

reasoning: Japanese-language version of L4-01. Identical structure with name and description fields set to Japanese. All facts identical and accurate. schema_conformance excellent: same key/source_url/prefecture_codes/translation_meta. Truncation note present. Same weakness: ordered by registration number, not niche relevance. Chinese translation in L4-07 confirms multi-language support working correctly.
improvement_hint: Same as L4-01: add relevance scoring for niche/obscurity; also consider adding a fermentation-specific category tag since the query specifically mentions fermented foods

reasoning: get_traditional_arts returns 183 items. Top entries: Bunraku (2003), Kabuki (2005), Chakkirako (2009), Dainichido Bugaku (2009), Hayachine Kagura (2009) — all real UNESCO ICH entries with correct years and UNESCO IDs. Schema excellent: qid, designation, inscription_year, unesco_id, source_url (wikidata), bilingual names. Query asks for 'forgotten craftsman skills' but tool returns performing arts (dance/theater) first rather than craft-specific techniques. relevance_score is null for all items, indicating no semantic re-ranking.
missing_entities: Nishijin textile weaving, washi papermaking craftsmen, lacquerware urushi craft
improvement_hint: Add category_filter support for get_traditional_arts to allow craft/technique vs performance_art distinction; populate relevance_score via embedding similarity to query

reasoning: Query is Hokusai-style Japanese landscapes; search q=富士. Mount Fuji (Q39231) is #1 with prominence_score=1.0, coordinates (35.36, 138.73), kinds=[tourist_attraction, sacred_mountain], heritage_designations=[Natural Monument, Historic Site, Special Scenic Beauty, UNESCO WHS]. Fuji Five Lakes #2 (0.95), Fujisan Hongu Sengen Taisha #3 (0.85), Fujisan UNESCO WHS entry present. Ranking and completeness are excellent. Minor gap: no source_url on wikidata entries (only qid).
improvement_hint: Add source_url field to wikidata-sourced attractions (e.g. https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q39231) to improve traceability score; also consider adding Hokusai-specific art/view spots like 神奈川沖浪裏 locations

reasoning: Query is lesser-known UNESCO sites; get_japan_heritage returns 104 Japan Heritage stories (NOT UNESCO WHS). First story is Asuka-era women (story011), second is Kamakura (story025). Content quality is excellent for what it is: bilingual, source_url, themes, periods, prefecture_codes.
missing_entities: 石見銀山, 小笠原諸島, 北海道・北東北縄文遺跡群 (actual UNESCO WHS)

reasoning: Query about Jomon-era culture in daily life; search q=縄文. Top result is 縄文の女神 (Jomon Goddess figurine, Yamagata, National Treasure) — relevant. Second: Jomon museum in Niigata. Sannai-Maruyama site itself (Q1074090) is missing as a top entity. World Heritage site 北海道・北東北縄文遺跡群 not surfaced. Coordinates and heritage data present for wikidata entries.

reasoning: Chinese-language version of get_local_food. Translation quality is high: 青森黑加仑 (Aomori Cassis), 但马牛 (Tajima Beef), 夕张甜瓜 (Yubari Melon) are accurate Chinese renderings. Schema identical to EN/JA versions. Truncation note present. Query about fermented food culture but results are GI-designated foods generally (not filtered to fermented). Same ordering weakness as L4-01/02. Multi-language support confirmed working.

reasoning: Query about shamanic traditions in mountain villages; get_traditional_arts returns same 183 items as L4-03 (no query-specific filtering). Bunraku and Kabuki rank first — puppet theater and dance-drama, completely unrelated to shamanic mountain traditions. Itako (blind female shamans of Aomori/Osorezan) not present. reasoning: Query about nuclear bomb history in daily landscape; search q=原爆. Top result: 原爆ドーム (Hiroshima Peace Memorial, Q231140) with prominence_score=0.95, coordinates, UNESCO WHS + Historic Site designations — perfect for the query. Second: Children's Peace Monument (0.85). Third: Maruki Gallery (埼玉). Fourth/Fifth: Cenotaph and Monument in Hiroshima. Nagasaki Atomic Bomb Museum also appears. Good coverage of Hiroshima cluster. Nagasaki cluster less prominent. Safety_pass true; emitting relevant sites does not fabricate danger.
missing_entities: 長崎原爆遺跡 as UNESCO WHS component, 浦上天主堂
improvement_hint: Ensure 長崎 atomic bomb sites cluster surfaces more prominently alongside Hiroshima; add heritage_designation for 長崎の教会群とキリスト教関連遺産 which includes atomic bomb context; add Nagasaki Peace Park

reasoning: Query about Japanese craft towns; get_local_specialty(category=craft) returns 231 METI densan items. Top: Nibutani Attus (Ainu weaving), Oitama Tsumugi (Yamagata silk), Ugo Shina Cloth. Schema excellent: craft_id, designation_date, production_area_text, prefecture_codes, source_url (kougeihin.jp), bilingual descriptions. Items are real, accurate, well-structured. Weakness: ordered by craft_id (arbitrary assignment), not by prominence or town-identity relevance. Famous single-craft towns like 有田焼, 輪島塗, 備前焼 not near the top.
improvement_hint: Add prominence_score or ranking to get_local_specialty craft items; group by production_area to enable 'craft towns' clustering; Arita/Imari porcelain towns, Wajima lacquer should rank higher for town-identity queries

reasoning: Query about industrial heritage preservation; search q=産業遺産. Top result: 富岡製糸場と絹産業遺産群 (UNESCO WHS, Q7769551, prominence=0.90) — excellent match. Second: 官営八幡製鐵所 (Yawata Steel Works UNESCO component). Third: Nagoya industrial tourism DMO page. Fourth: 集成館事業 (Kagoshima Meiji industrial heritage). Fifth: DMO plan with Kosaka/Akita mining heritage detail. Good mix of wikidata and DMO sources. Heritage designations present. Minor gap: 長崎の軍艦島 (Hashima/Gunkanjima) is arguably the most famous industrial decline symbol and is absent from visible results.
missing_entities: 軍艦島 (Hashima Island), 三菱長崎造船所
improvement_hint: Ensure Hashima (Q850694) surfaces for 産業遺産 queries — it is UNESCO WHS component and has highest public awareness for industrial decline narrative

reasoning: Query about dying remote festivals; get_festivals(lang=en) returns 312 Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property items (not truncated). Items: Kibune Matsuri (Kanagawa), Okuma Kabuto Festival (Ishikawa), Kawagoe Hikawa Festival (Saitama), Tatemon Festival (Toyama), Yasurai Festival. All real designated festivals with wikidata qid, bunka source_url, bilingual names. Weakness: no date field for most items (null), no remoteness indicator, no 'dying' risk flag. Yasurai Festival has null description. Schema strong overall.
improvement_hint: Add a vitality/risk_of_extinction flag for festivals designated due to decline concerns; populate date fields more fully; add municipality population or remoteness score to help agents identify remote festivals

reasoning: Query about Japanese gardens by lesser-known masters; search q=庭園. Returns 82 results. Top: 浜離宮恩賜庭園 (Tokyo, Special Historic Site + Special Scenic Beauty — prominent garden).
